{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "ClearSCADA is an industrial SCADA/ICS software that serves as the supervisory and control application in Industrial IoT deployments; it is purpose-built for IoT/OT environments and is an active element of such systems. The flaw lies in the application layer (improper authentication in the software).",
  "category": "controller",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerability affects the firmware of a Foscam indoor IP camera—an IoT device that functions as part of a consumer home-security system. The flaw resides in the device’s web management interface (firmware), placing it in the device layer of the IoT ecosystem.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The Honeywell Experion PKS modules are embedded industrial control components that form an active part of an Industrial IoT control system; the buffer-overflow flaws reside in the firmware/software of these devices, making them IoT-specific under the device layer definition.",
  "category": "industrial controller",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Suprema BioStar 2 is an access-control platform that manages network-connected biometric readers and door controllers; these devices are purpose-built IoT elements and the server software is an active component in that IoT system. The vulnerability is in the platform’s web application layer, fitting the application component.",
  "category": "access_control",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Bosch IP Helper is a dedicated utility used to discover/configure Bosch IP cameras (IoT devices). It is purpose-built for managing those devices, making it part of the IoT application layer. The DLL-search-path issue lies in the application itself, not in generic OS software, so it is IoT-specific.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "GE Proficy HMI/SCADA CIMPLICITY is purpose-built industrial control software that orchestrates and supervises field devices and PLCs in manufacturing environments, serving as an active functional element of an industrial IoT system; the vulnerability lies in its web application component, placing it in the application layer.",
  "category": "industrial_control",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "The vulnerable products (EcoStruxure Power Monitoring Expert, Energy Expert, Power SCADA Operation) are dedicated industrial power-monitoring/SCADA platforms that interface with field devices and sensors in power systems. They serve as orchestration and dashboard software for an IIoT deployment, fitting the application layer of the defined IoT ecosystem rather than being general-purpose software.",
  "category": "scada",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "B. Braun SpaceCom2 is a network-connected medical device module used with infusion systems; it is a purpose-built embedded device within the IoT ecosystem. The flaw is in firmware data authenticity (no cryptographic signatures), which is a device-layer issue.",
  "category": "medical device",
  "sector": "healthcare iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "communication",
  "reason": "Eclipse Mosquitto is an MQTT broker implementing the MQTT protocol, a purpose-built messaging protocol widely used for IoT communications. The vulnerability directly targets the broker software that enables data transfer between IoT devices and services, so it belongs to the IoT ecosystem’s communication layer.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "other"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Thermoscan IP is purpose-built software used to manage network-connected temperature sensors (Plug&Track data loggers). It is an integral, functional element of that IoT system, not general-purpose PC software. The flaw lies in the application’s installation folder permissions, so it maps to the application layer.",
  "category": "sensors",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerability affects ABB Panel Builder 600 firmware running an embedded FTP server on industrial operator panels, which are purpose-built embedded devices used within industrial control (IIoT) environments; the issue resides in the device’s firmware, fitting the device layer definition.",
  "category": "industrial control",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Triangle MicroWorks SCADA Data Gateway is specialized software used in industrial control/SCADA environments to manage and translate field device data, functioning as an IoT/OT gateway. It is purpose-built for IoT deployments (industrial) and is an active element in an IoT system, so it meets the IoT-specific rule. The vulnerability is in the application-level trusted-certificate feature, not in device firmware or low-level protocol stacks.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The affected products are standalone Dahua digital video recorder appliances used in IP camera surveillance systems. These are purpose-built embedded devices that form part of an IoT video-surveillance deployment; the vulnerability is in the firmware (hard-coded passwords), i.e., the device layer.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "OctoPrint is a purpose-built software platform that provides remote control and monitoring of network-connected 3D printers, which are IoT actuators. The vulnerability impacts the application layer managing these devices, so it directly affects an active element of an IoT system.",
  "category": "actuators",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "Cisco Webex Desk Hub is a dedicated network-connected telepresence endpoint running RoomOS/CE firmware; it is a purpose-built smart device integral to an IoT videoconferencing setup, and the vulnerability lies in its on-device firmware.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "other"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "communication",
  "reason": "The Silicon Labs Z/IP Gateway SDK is a purpose-built IoT protocol stack for Z-Wave devices, enabling communication between Z-Wave end devices and IP networks. The flaw involves malformed Z-Wave command classes that disrupt acknowledgments, indicating a vulnerability in the IoT communication layer. The product is an IoT gateway, not general-purpose hardware or traditional networking gear.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerable product (QCP 200W camera firmware) is an Internet-connected camera that streams video over RTSP, fitting the device layer of the IoT ecosystem; the issue stems from insufficient authentication in the firmware, so it directly affects an IoT device rather than general-purpose IT equipment.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerability resides in the firmware of network-connected surveillance cameras—purpose-built IoT devices that function as active elements in an IoT video-surveillance system. The issue is firmware-level (device layer) stack-based buffer overflow, making it an IoT device-layer vulnerability.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The Siemens CP 1604 and CP 1616 are specialized industrial communication processor modules used within SIMATIC automation environments. They are purpose-built hardware with dedicated firmware and an embedded web server, functioning as active elements in industrial IoT systems rather than generic IT infrastructure. The CSRF flaw resides in the firmware’s web interface, so the issue maps to the device layer of the IoT ecosystem.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "onion-oled-js is a Node.js library created specifically for Onion Omega IoT modules to drive an OLED display. The product is purpose-built for IoT hardware and is an active element in an IoT deployment. The issue exists in the library’s scroll() API (application layer).",
  "category": "actuator",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "UniFi Protect is purpose-built software that manages UniFi IP cameras/NVR devices; it is an active element of a video-surveillance IoT system. The flaw is in the web application logic (information disclosure), so it belongs to the application layer.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The affected product is a Vivotek PT7135 IP camera—an IoT device that collects video data and connects to a network. The directory-traversal flaw lies in the camera’s firmware, placing it in the device layer of the IoT ecosystem. The product is purpose-built for IoT and functions as an active element of an IoT system, so the CVE is IoT-specific.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerability affects Visteon automotive infotainment firmware, a purpose-built embedded system within vehicles that functions as an active element in an automotive IoT environment. The flaw lies in the firmware update mechanism of the MCU, placing it in the device layer.",
  "category": "infotainment system",
  "sector": "automative iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerability is in the firmware of UNIMO UDR-JA16xx digital video recorder devices, which are purpose-built, network-connected hardware elements used in CCTV systems; this fits the device layer of the IoT ecosystem.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "smart cities iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "JerryScript is an ultra-lightweight JavaScript engine explicitly designed to run on resource-constrained embedded devices and microcontrollers that form the device layer of IoT systems; the vulnerability is therefore in IoT-specific technology and affects code executing on the devices themselves.",
  "category": "other",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "WAGO PFC100/200 are industrial PLC devices that directly control/monitor field equipment and are integral components of Industrial IoT deployments; the vulnerability is in the device firmware (iocheckd service), thus targeting the device layer.",
  "category": "industrial controller",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Inductive Automation Ignition is an industrial automation SCADA/IIoT gateway and orchestration platform purpose-built to control and manage industrial devices via OPC-UA. It functions as an active element in an industrial IoT deployment rather than as general-purpose software, so the CVE is IoT-specific. As a local controller/gateway that sits above field hardware, it belongs to the application layer.",
  "category": "gateway",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "nonIoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "NI SystemLink Server and FlexLogger are general-purpose test and measurement management applications that run on conventional computers/servers; the description gives no clear evidence that the vulnerable software itself is a purpose-built, active element inside an IoT system, so it is classified as non-IoT.",
  "category": "platform",
  "sector": "other"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Tollgrade LightHouse SMS is management software for Tollgrade’s LightHouse smart-grid sensors; it is purpose-built for monitoring and controlling IoT sensor devices in a utility deployment. The vulnerability is in the authentication logic of this software, placing it in the application layer of the IoT stack.",
  "category": "sensors",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "Logitech Harmony Hub is a purpose-built consumer smart-home hub (an active element of an IoT system). The flaw is in its firmware, i.e., the device layer, allowing OS command injection.",
  "category": "gateway",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Delta Electronics DIAScreen is specialized HMI/SCADA software used to operate and monitor industrial automation devices, making it a purpose-built element within an industrial IoT environment rather than general-purpose desktop software.   "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The Moxa MGate MB3xxx products are industrial protocol gateway devices specifically designed to interconnect field devices (e.g., Modbus serial equipment) with Ethernet/IP networks in industrial environments. They are purpose-built elements of an IoT/IIoT deployment and their firmware stores configuration passwords in cleartext, which is a vulnerability located in the device’s software/firmware layer.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "communication",
  "reason": "The vulnerability is in Qualcomm modem/SoC firmware that is explicitly marketed for Consumer IoT, Industrial IoT, Auto, and Wearables.   "category": "chipsets",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "communication",
  "reason": "IndraDrive is an industrial motor drive, a purpose-built IoT/OT device used in factory automation. The flaw is in its PROFINET protocol stack, a network-communication component, and a crafted UDP message can render the drive unresponsive. Hence it is an IoT vulnerability in the communication layer.",
  "category": "actuator",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerability is in firmware of Qualcomm Snapdragon SoCs that include purpose-built lines explicitly branded for Consumer IoT and Industrial IoT. These chipsets are active hardware elements inside IoT devices; therefore the flaw directly impacts the IoT device layer.",
  "category": "chipsets",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",

The flaw is in the application’s authentication logic, directly impacting access to IoT camera functions, so it is IoT-specific.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerable products are network-connected power meters (purpose-built IoT devices used for energy monitoring). The issue is an SSH key embedded in the device firmware, so the flaw resides in the device layer.",

}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerable products are Pepperl+Fuchs ICDM-RX industrial protocol converter/gateway devices that sit in operational technology networks to bridge field-bus protocols such as Modbus, Profinet, and Ethernet/IP.   "sector": "industrial iot"

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",

}

{
  "label": "IoT",

  "reason": "The affected items are network-enabled HP printers and digital senders that run embedded firmware and use the LLMNR protocol for name resolution. {
